summary: Frontend-only landing page verification for PlanMove. All 8 requested behaviors verified via Playwright at 1440x900. All CTA hover behaviors (no translate, color transitions #25D366 -> #168222 over 500ms), Trust Bar structure/icons, dark footer, hero integrity, sticky problem cards 01-07, and Lenis-based navigation all confirmed working. Scroll performance is acceptable (4 long tasks >50ms, max 77ms — well under the ~5 threshold).
critical_code_review_comments: Lenis console warning: 'Please ensure that the container has a non-static position, like relative/fixed/absolute'. Consider adding position:relative to the lenis wrapper container to remove the warning.

Nav order observation: clicking nav-loesungen scrolls to y=1880, then nav-vorteile scrolls back UP to y=956. This is correct if Vorteile section is above Lösungen in the DOM, but review whether the header nav order matches the on-page section order for a better UX.

Hero H1 headline in current build reads 'Weniger Büro. Mehr Zeit. Mehr Kunden. Mehr Umsatz.' — earlier the review request implied a Mehr Kunden/Mehr Zeit/Mehr Geld/Weniger Stress variant appears initially (before scroll). Confirmed both variants render in different visits; verify this is intentional (A/B or animated headline).

verified_checks.cta_hover_no_transform: all 4 CTAs computed transform:none before AND after hover ✓
verified_checks.page_height: 9195px, smooth-scrolled top-to-bottom in ~4s without visible freezes ✓
verified_checks.navigation_all_4_nav_links_scroll: nav-probleme, nav-loesungen, nav-vorteile, nav-ueber all trigger scroll ✓

context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend-only SPA. Lenis smooth scroll used — always use window.__lenis.scrollTo instead of window.scrollTo. When testing CTA 'no movement on hover' use computed transform and page-relative (offsetTop) coordinates rather than getBoundingClientRect alone, since Lenis may still be animating and scrollY changes cause false-positive dy drift in bounding boxes. All data-testids listed in the review request are present and functional.
